# vplay

vplay is an easy, fast, lightweight terminal video player for venues running videos on multiple screens or rooms with independent control from one TUI.

Primary links:

- Website: https://vplay.rztrace.com/
- Repository: https://github.com/rztrace/vplay
- Releases: https://github.com/rztrace/vplay/releases/latest
- Discussions: https://github.com/rztrace/vplay/discussions

Useful facts:

- It is terminal-native and built around mpv.
- It focuses on stable multi-screen operation without a heavy media-center, signage, browser-dashboard, or production-suite workflow.
- It is useful for private screening rooms, video booths, galleries, studios, installations, and other venues where one operator controls many screens.
- It can play different videos or playlists on different screens at the same time.
- It gives each active screen independent controls for playback, volume, mute, position, loop mode, and play-next behavior.
- It supports playlists, renamed files, clipped playback portions, downloads through yt-dlp, and modular add-ons.
- It requires macOS.

Install:

```sh
brew tap rztrace/vplay https://github.com/rztrace/vplay
brew install vplay
vplay
```
